close_reason: get_cached_or_scan now returns cached NVM ID without metadata round-trip
description: find_cached_or_scan (lib.rs:369-394) validates a cached id by calling client.nvm_metadata(cached) on every call. For a read-heavy workload over a remote (TCP/UDS) HSM this is one full round-trip per get(), so the cache only saves the O(n) scan, not the RPC. Trigger: an application that polls a wolfHSM-backed credential frequently — every poll incurs a metadata RPC. Trade-off the code makes is correctness (other clients may have rotated the object) over latency. Mitigation options: (a) add a configurable cache TTL so reads within TTL skip validation, (b) document that get() is one round-trip and refresh() forces a full re-scan, (c) accept and document as defensible. This is a P2 efficiency note rather than a bug — current behavior is correct, just chatty.
title: Every get() costs an nvm_metadata round-trip even on cache hit
